proposals.architecture_gap: The system has specifications and validators but no executable orchestrator, no runtime state, and no swarm activation path.
proposals.data_model_changes: Introduce machine-readable CSSM snapshots, runtime status manifests, and initialization metadata.
proposals.governance_implications: Councils remain authoritative; runtime merely routes proposals and records decisions.
proposals.invention_concept: Preserve the repo's document canon as GCML while layering a deterministic local coordination kernel on top of it.
proposals.migration_strategy: Non-breaking overlay. Existing docs remain authoritative while runtime files are introduced under /runtime.
proposals.problem_statement: The system cannot execute recursive architecture evolution as a program.
proposals.proposed_architecture_change: Add a local bootstrap kernel that loads the AAS spec pack, materializes configuration, builds CSSM snapshots, and treats repo documentation as the canonical memory substrate.
proposals.protocol_implications: No shared repo workflow breaks; runtime derives state from existing docs without replacing them.
proposals.security_implications: Keeps execution local and file-backed. No new external attack surface is introduced.
proposals.title: Bootstrap Coordination Kernel Overlay

proposals.architecture_gap: The current system cannot allocate capabilities dynamically, route proposals structurally, or coordinate execution through machine-readable task dependencies.
proposals.data_model_changes: Add agent profiles, proposal registry entries, task dependency nodes, and council decision records.
proposals.governance_implications: Council decisions gain durable machine-readable records for arbitration and replay.
proposals.invention_concept: Convert the AAS from a document-only operating manual into a governed execution system with ACR, proposal registry, and task graph surfaces.
proposals.migration_strategy: Use bootstrap registries first; evolve toward a persistent service surface later without invalidating stored state.
proposals.problem_statement: Capability-aware assignment and proposal-driven execution were impossible.
proposals.proposed_architecture_change: Introduce a bootstrap ACR, proposal registry, council decision records, and task graph snapshots under /runtime/state.
proposals.protocol_implications: Proposal routing becomes deterministic and auditable. Task generation becomes machine-readable rather than purely narrative.
proposals.security_implications: Stronger traceability improves auditability and post-failure reconstruction.
proposals.title: Stateful Registry and Task Graph Core

proposals.architecture_gap: Recursive evolution requires persistent event history, swarm isolation, telemetry, and deterministic recovery. None of those existed operationally.
proposals.data_model_changes: Add event logs, knowledge graph edges for proposals/tasks/councils, and recovery manifests that point to deterministic replay sources.
proposals.governance_implications: Governance evolves from prose rules into executable review surfaces while still preserving human override.
proposals.invention_concept: Promote AAS into an event-sourced recursive control plane where swarms, councils, proposals, and recovery are first-class runtime entities.
proposals.migration_strategy: Begin with local JSONL/event-store files and expand toward service-backed components only after the file-backed control plane proves stable.
proposals.problem_statement: The current AAS can design Atrahasis artifacts, but it cannot recursively redesign itself as a continuously improving architecture evolution engine.
proposals.proposed_architecture_change: Adopt event-sourced telemetry logs as the runtime backbone, generate isolated swarm CSSM branches, and use council outcomes to synthesize evolved canonical state versions.
proposals.protocol_implications: Architecture evolution becomes an explicit workflow with replayable decisions, not just a narrative instruction set.
proposals.security_implications: Recovery and audit improve because all critical decisions become append-only telemetry events.
proposals.title: Event-Sourced Recursive Evolution Control Plane

proposals.architecture_gap: The current runtime can bootstrap and resume, but it still needs sustained self-improvement cycles to become a recursive architecture evolution engine.
proposals.data_model_changes: Add cycle-scoped state, evolution deltas, and replayable improvement history to the runtime state graph.
proposals.governance_implications: Councils gain repeated review cadence and explicit cycle history.
proposals.invention_concept: Extract the bootstrap overlay into a long-running local coordination service without breaking the document canon.
proposals.migration_strategy: Adopt cycle outputs incrementally by synthesizing a new canonical CSSM version after each meta-evaluation.
proposals.problem_statement: Pending architecture evolution pressure remains on: Refactor bootstrap kernel into a long-running coordination service, Add executable council policy modules and replay-based recovery.
proposals.proposed_architecture_change: Extract the bootstrap overlay into a long-running local coordination service without breaking the document canon.
proposals.protocol_implications: Proposal routing and council replay remain file-backed but gain persistent evolution cadence.
proposals.security_implications: No external network surface is added; the improvement loop remains local and auditable through telemetry.

proposals.protocol_implications: The AAS evolves via explicit, repeatable, proposal-driven cycles rather than one-shot bootstrap logic.
proposals.security_implications: Replayable cycle history improves auditability and recovery confidence.
